summary: Added a compact Control Runway to the first-screen Company State panel so the default deck shows game-like lane levels, gate pressure, staged activity, and Command-deck drill-through without increasing long-scroll pressure.

behavior: Renders the selected lane plus the highest-pressure active lanes as compact level nodes.
Shows active, gate, and staged counts derived from existing snapshot/browser-local data.
Routes runway node clicks into the Command deck and opens the lane detail panel.
Uses internal horizontal runway scrolling on small screens to avoid increasing page-level scroll and overflow.

browser.finding: Control Runway rendered 6 level nodes, exactly 1 active node, 3 visible Control deck sections, 0 document horizontal overflow, and no console warnings/errors.
Runway node click switched to the Command deck, opened the lane detail panel, updated the hash to view=trail&deck=command, and produced no console warnings/errors.
Responsive QA at 390x844, 820x1180, and 1280x720 showed visible runway, 6 nodes, 0 page-level horizontal overflow, and no console warnings/errors.
Small-screen runway was changed from a stacked grid to contained internal horizontal scrolling after QA found the stacked version added avoidable scroll height.

follow_up: Add generated texture/sprite treatment to the Control Runway if the next iteration focuses on visual polish assets.
Add similar compact level tracks to Worlds and History decks without increasing default scroll.
Continue replacing passive summaries with direct game-style interactions and deck-preserving route transitions.
